root_cause: Service mesh authorization policy denied app-to-app traffic
evidence: Pod shop/frontend-api-5c6f7d7f9b-s8k2m uses service-mesh sidecar 'istio-proxy' for app-to-app authorization
Observed 3 mesh authorization denial occurrence(s) within 20 minutes

Representative mesh authorization denial: Envoy RBAC: access denied by Istio AuthorizationPolicy deny-frontend-to-reviews-v1 for outbound GET http://reviews.shop.svc.cluster.local:9080/v1/reviews from principal cluster.local/ns/shop/sa/frontend-api
Mesh authorization policy object 'deny-frontend-to-reviews-v1' is present for this workload path

Representative application impact after mesh denial: Readiness probe failed: GET http://localhost:8080/ready returned 503 because upstream reviews.shop.svc.cluster.local:9080 returned 403 Forbidden: RBAC: access denied
Mesh authorization denials persisted for 2.0 minutes
Destination Service 'reviews' has 2 ready endpoint(s), making a Kubernetes NetworkPolicy or backend outage less likely

causes.message: Pod traffic is evaluated by service-mesh sidecar 'istio-proxy' before reaching peer workloads
A service-mesh authorization policy denies the app-to-app request after network connectivity succeeds
The denial is explicit mesh RBAC/authorization feedback rather than a CNI NetworkPolicy timeout
Application health checks or upstream calls fail because the mesh returns authorization denial

pod:frontend-api-5c6f7d7f9b-s8k2m: Application traffic reaches the mesh proxy but is denied by mesh authorization
container:istio-proxy: Envoy RBAC: access denied by Istio AuthorizationPolicy deny-frontend-to-reviews-v1 for outbound GET http://reviews.shop.svc.cluster.local:9080/v1/reviews from principal cluster.local/ns/shop/sa/frontend-api
timeline:mesh_authorization: 3 authorization denial occurrence(s) in the recent timeline
authorizationpolicy:deny-frontend-to-rev: Mesh policy is the authorization layer implicated by the denial
container:frontend-api: Readiness probe failed: GET http://localhost:8080/ready returned 503 because upstream reviews.shop.svc.cluster.local:9080 returned 403 Forbidden: RBAC: access denied
service:reviews: 2 ready endpoint(s) available while mesh authorization denies traffic

likely_causes: Istio AuthorizationPolicy, Linkerd policy, or Consul intention denies the source identity, method, path, or destination
The workload service account or SPIFFE identity does not match the mesh policy principal selector
A recent mesh policy rollout introduced a DENY rule or removed an ALLOW rule for this app-to-app path
Kubernetes Services and endpoints are healthy, but L7 mesh authorization rejects the request
suggested_checks: Inspect mesh AuthorizationPolicy/ServerAuthorization/intentions for source principal, namespace, method, and path selectors
Check mesh proxy access logs for RBAC denied or HTTP 403 responses on the failing route
Verify this is not Kubernetes NetworkPolicy by testing DNS and TCP reachability separately from the HTTP request
